chapter: Chapter: The Amazing World of Solutes, Solvents, and Solutions

questions.question: Why is salt water considered a homogeneous mixture?
questions.answer: Because salt particles are evenly distributed throughout the water.

questions.question: Why is nitrogen considered the solvent in air?
questions.answer: Because it is present in the largest proportion.

questions.question: Why does stirring help a solute dissolve faster?
questions.answer: It increases contact between solute and solvent particles.

questions.question: Why does heating water increase solubility of many solids?
questions.answer: Higher temperature increases particle motion allowing more solute to dissolve.

questions.question: Why does gas solubility decrease at higher temperatures?
questions.answer: Gas particles escape more easily when temperature increases.

questions.question: Why do fish struggle in warm polluted water?
questions.answer: Warm water holds less dissolved oxygen.

questions.question: Why does oil float on water but not mix with it?
questions.answer: Oil has lower density and is immiscible with water.

questions.question: Why do objects with higher density than water sink?
questions.answer: Their mass per unit volume is greater than water.

questions.question: Why can large ships float even though steel sinks in water?
questions.answer: Ships have hollow structures that reduce overall density.

questions.question: Why is density called a characteristic property?
questions.answer: Because each substance has a specific density value.

questions.question: How can density help identify unknown materials?
questions.answer: By comparing measured density with known values.

questions.question: Why does heating usually decrease density of substances?
questions.answer: Particles move apart increasing volume.

questions.question: Why does hot air rise in the atmosphere?
questions.answer: Because hot air is less dense than cold air.

questions.question: Why are gases more compressible than solids?
questions.answer: Gas particles are far apart with large empty spaces.

questions.question: Why must measurements be taken at eye level in a measuring cylinder?
questions.answer: To avoid parallax error.

questions.question: Why are measuring cylinders tall and narrow?
questions.answer: To improve accuracy of volume measurements.

questions.question: Why is the displacement method useful for irregular objects?
questions.answer: Because their volume cannot be measured using geometric formulas.

questions.question: Why does ice float though it is made of water?
questions.answer: Ice has lower density due to expansion during freezing.

questions.question: Why does increasing pressure increase density of gases?
questions.answer: Pressure pushes gas particles closer together.

questions.question: Why is dissolved oxygen important in aquatic ecosystems?
questions.answer: It supports respiration of aquatic organisms.

questions.question: Why is density important in science experiments?
questions.answer: It helps determine physical properties of substances.

questions.question: Why does adding salt to water increase its density?
questions.answer: Because dissolved salt increases mass without much change in volume.

questions.question: Why do hot air balloons rise in air?
questions.answer: Because heated air inside the balloon is less dense.

questions.question: Why do scientists measure both mass and volume when studying substances?
questions.answer: To calculate density.

questions.question: Why are solutions important in medicine preparation?
questions.answer: Because medicines must dissolve uniformly for proper dosage.
